nativeRange.habitat: Coral reefs, inshore grassy and rocky areas; found from shallow depths down to about 40 meters, most commonly 2–18 m.

diet.notes: Primarily grazes on filamentous algae and macroalgae. In captivity, tends to thrive on dried seaweed (nori) offered several times per week, along with spirulina-based foods and commercially formulated herbivore pellets. Has been documented accepting meaty foods when housed with carnivorous tankmates, though plant matter should remain the dietary focus.

imageCandidates.notes: Photo by G. P. Schmahl, NOAA / Flower Garden Banks National Marine Sanctuary; school of blue tang at the Flower Garden Banks, Gulf of Mexico, 2007. Full resolution 2645×1759 px. Shows adult coloration clearly.

Thumbnail from FishBase species summary page for Acanthurus coeruleus (adult specimen). License not explicitly stated on the thumbnail URL; full license info would be on the FishBase photo detail page.
Product photo from LiveAquaria. URL inferred from product page pattern; may need verification. Typical retailer photography with clean composition.

summary: The Blue Caribbean Tang (Acanthurus coeruleus) is a vibrant Atlantic reef fish that generally does well in established reef aquariums. Adults are known for their striking blue coloration; juveniles start bright yellow and transition through a blue-tailed phase before reaching adult color.
careNotes:
The Blue Caribbean Tang is commonly considered an intermediate-level species due to its size and space requirements, and its reported sensitivity to poor water quality and transport stress. It tends to do best in well-established tanks with plentiful algae growth on live rock, generally advised to be set up for at least 6–12 months before introduction.

Minimum tank size recommendations vary considerably across sources: AquaHoy cites 300 L (80 gal), LiveAquaria recommends 180 gal (~681 L), and ReefApp specifies 900 L — a spread exceeding 3×. The averaged value of approximately 627 L has been used, but reviewers should note this wide disagreement. For adults reaching full size (~39 cm), larger tanks are likely preferable.

Adult size similarly varies: LiveAquaria lists up to 9 inches (23 cm), while FishBase and Wikipedia document a maximum of 39 cm TL with a common length of 25 cm. The species appears to reach smaller sizes in aquarium conditions than in the wild.

Temperature was averaged from three sources: LiveAquaria (22.2–25.6°C from 72–78°F), FishBase (23.7–28.1°C), and AquaHoy (24–28°C), yielding a recommended range of approximately 23–27°C.

Lifespan: most aquarium sources report 10–15 years with good care; one source (AquaHoy) cited up to 47 years, which appears to be an outlier and has not been used.

This species has caudal-peduncle spines (scalpels) that can inflict painful wounds — standard surgeonfish handling precautions apply. It has been known to act as a cleaner fish for sea turtles in the wild, picking parasites from their skin. Juveniles are a distinct bright yellow with blue highlights and are sometimes sold as a separate color morph.

The species is generally considered reef-safe with no documented coral or invertebrate harm. It tends to be semi-aggressive primarily toward other tangs and surgeonfish, especially conspecifics, and is best kept singly unless the tank is very large.
breedingNotes: Acanthurus coeruleus has not been successfully bred in home aquariums and is generally considered impractical for captive reproduction. In the wild, the species forms resident spawning aggregations, typically in late afternoon following high tide. Captive breeding is not documented in the hobbyist literature.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Product page extracted via the tangs-surgeons collection. Provided adult size (9 in), tank size (180 gal), temperature (72–78°F), pH (8.1–8.4), salinity (1.020–1.025), diet, temperament, and reef-safe status.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed taxonomy (Acanthuridae, Acanthuriformes), maximum length (39.0 cm TL), native range (Western Atlantic), temperature range (23.7–28.1°C), habitat (coral reefs and grassy/rocky inshore areas), diet (algae), and behavioral notes on caudal spine and cleaning behavior.

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Cross-verified adult size (35 cm), temperature (~26°C), salinity (~1.026 SG), minimum tank size (900 L), reef-safe status, and conspecific aggression notes. Tank size of 900 L is notably higher than other sources.

additional.name: AquaHoy
additional.notes: Additional cross-verification of temperature (24–28°C), pH (8.1–8.4), salinity (1.020–1.025), minimum tank (300 L / 80 gal). Also noted aquarium size typically 20–25 cm versus wild 39 cm. Cited lifespan of 47 years treated as outlier.

fish.breedingNotes: Not bred in home aquariums. Forms spawning aggregations in the wild during late afternoon tidal cycles.
